text: Handling deadlines well is an essential skill in both academic and professional settings. Good time management helps you break large projects into smaller, manageable tasks. Prioritization is equally important: you should identify which tasks are most urgent or important and tackle those first. Scope management means knowing when to say "no" to extra work that could derail your schedule. Finally, clear communication with your team or supervisor can prevent misunderstandings. If you foresee a delay, let others know early so you can find a solution together. By combining these strategies, you can meet deadlines with less stress and better results.
